name: wireless-driver-control-frame-audit
description: Systematic audit methodology for wireless driver control-frame and Information Element (IE) parsing across Linux kernel drivers and equivalents. Use when: (1) auditing a vendor's WLAN driver for memory-safety bugs in beacon/probe-resp/assoc-frame parsing, (2) hunting for cross-vendor bug-class propagation (idioms copy-pasted across drivers), (3) mapping coverage gaps in helper-vs-raw-walk discipline, (4) pivoting from finding a single bug to finding its peers tree-wide. Codifies bug-class taxonomy (TLV underflow, vendor-IE deref-before-bound, edge-byte OOB, per-EID typed-pointer cast / WiFiTaco class), the inconsistency-within-driver heuristic, and the cross-OS protocol-parser-surface mapping that turns one driver's findings into multi-platform impact.
